name: calculate_position_size
description: Calculate the number of shares to buy based on risk management rules. Uses the fixed-risk method: risk_amount = cash * risk_percent, shares = risk_amount / (entry_price - stop_loss_price).

risk_percent.description: Max percentage of cash to risk on this trade (e.g., 0.02 for 2%)

name: calculate_risk_reward
description: Calculate the risk-to-reward ratio for a trade setup. A ratio >= 2.0 is generally considered favorable.

stop_loss_price.description: Stop-loss price for the trade (alias: stop_loss)

name: calculate_stop_loss
description: Calculate a stop-loss price based on ATR (Average True Range) volatility. stop_loss = entry_price - (ATR * multiplier).

name: escalate_to_pm
description: Immediately escalate the cycle to the Board of Directors and Portfolio Manager, bypass standard research loops, and submit the reasoning/findings.

reason.description: The critical reason or justification for escalation.

name: get_institutional_holdings
description: Get institutional hedge fund ownership data for a stock. Shows which top hedge funds hold it, position sizes, new positions, quarterly momentum, and whether top-performing funds are invested.

name: get_market_map_data
description: Fetches the top movers and market map data for S&P 500 components, grouped by sector. Use this to analyze broad market trends and sector performance for the day.

name: get_portfolio_state
description: Get the bot's full portfolio state including cash balance, all open positions with P&L, sector breakdown, and position count. Use this to understand portfolio health and make informed BUY/SELL decisions.

name: get_position_pnl
description: Check the P&L and health of a specific held position. Returns entry price, current price, unrealized P&L, holding duration, and stop-loss proximity. Use this during debates to evaluate whether to hold or sell.

name: get_ticker_summary
description: Get a one-call fundamentals snapshot for a ticker: name, sector/industry, market cap, P/E and forward P/E, 52-week range, average volume, dividend yield, analyst price target, and next earnings date.

name: lazy_web_search
description: Search the web for information using a controlled, truncated search engine.

name: read_user_notes
description: Read markdown notes written by the human user. These notes contain human thoughts, context, watchlists, or market observations. Always check this if the user tells you they 'wrote it down' or if you need extra context.

filename.description: Optional. The specific .md filename to read. If omitted, returns a list of all available notes.

name: request_peer_analysis
description: Request a specific peer agent (e.g. 'quant_analyst', 'fundamental_analyst', 'junior_analyst') to run a query, check a lead, or verify details, and post findings to the whiteboard.

target_agent.description: The exact name of the target agent (junior_analyst, fundamental_analyst, quant_analyst).

query.description: The specific question or instructions for the target agent.

name: run_backtest
description: Backtest a named equation from the library over historical price data. Returns performance metrics: PnL, win rate, max drawdown, Sharpe ratio, and trade list.

equation_name.description: Name of the equation from the library to backtest.

parameters.description: JSON string of parameters for the backtest.

name: run_equation
description: Execute a mathematical equation against live price/technical data for a ticker. You can run a named equation from the library OR provide raw Python code. The code receives 'df' (DataFrame with OHLCV + technicals) and 'params' (dict). Must assign output to 'result'.

ticker.description: Stock ticker to run against (e.g., AAPL).

equation_name.description: Name of a saved equation. If empty, 'code' must be provided.

code.description: Raw Python code if not using a named equation.

name: save_equation
description: Save a new mathematical equation to the shared Quant Equation Library. The equation must be Python code that receives 'df' (price DataFrame) and 'params' (dict), and assigns its output to 'result'. Future agents can reuse this equation.

name.description: Unique snake_case name for the equation.

code.description: Python code. Must assign output to 'result'. Has access to df, params, np, pd.

description.description: What this equation does and when to use it.

name: save_trading_chart
description: Render and save an HTML chart and JSON overlay data from the technical analyst agent.

overlays.description: JSON overlays array from the agent

period.description: Timeframe of historical data to fetch (e.g., '1mo', '3mo', '6mo', '1y')

name: search_equations
description: Search the shared Quant Equation Library for existing mathematical strategies. Returns equations with their win rates, Sharpe ratios, and code. Use this before writing a new equation to check if one already exists.

query.description: Search keyword (e.g., 'zscore', 'momentum', 'rsi'). Empty returns top equations by win rate.

name: whiteboard_annotate
description: Add a note or comment to an existing whiteboard entry without overwriting it. Use this to highlight risks, disagree with a consensus, or add context.

entry_id.description: The exact entry_id (from whiteboard_read) to annotate.

author.description: YOUR agent name (e.g. v3_quant_analyst) so teammates know who wrote this. Always provide it.

name: whiteboard_read
description: Read a specific section of the team's shared whiteboard for a given ticker. The whiteboard is a living document where agents collaborate. Sections include: 'market_context', 'risk_flags', 'signals', 'consensus', 'trade_plan'.

ticker.description: The stock ticker to read from.

name: whiteboard_summarize
description: Get a full summary of all sections currently on the whiteboard. Provides a snapshot of the entire team's consensus and state.

name: whiteboard_write
description: Write or overwrite a section of the team's shared whiteboard. Use this to post your final analysis or consensus for other agents to read. Writing will bump the version number of the section. Content should be valid JSON string.

ticker.description: The stock ticker this relates to.

section.description: The section name to write to (e.g. 'consensus').

content.description: The content to write (preferably a JSON string or clear text).

author.description: YOUR agent name (e.g. v3_quant_analyst) so teammates know who wrote this. Always provide it.
